name: align_exceeds_input
code:
proof fn phi_1_align_exceeds_input(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) > a,
{
}
reason: Downward alignment must never return a value exceeding the original input

name: align_to_one_changes_value
code:
proof fn phi_2_align_to_one_changes_value(a: usize)
    requires
        a < usize::MAX,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, 1) != a,
{
}
reason: Aligning to 1 should be a no-op since every integer is 1-aligned; a changed value would indicate a broken spec

name: nonzero_align_stays_nonzero
code:
proof fn phi_3_nonzero_align_stays_nonzero(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        a > 0,
        b > 1,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) > 0,
{
}
reason: A nonzero address aligned down can legitimately become zero (e.g., a=1 b=4096); if the spec claims otherwise it is too strong

name: aligned_additive_divisors
code:
proof fn phi_4_aligned_additive_divisors(addr: nat, s1: nat, s2: nat)
    requires
        s1 > 0,
        s2 > 0,
        aligned(addr, s1),
        aligned(addr, s2),
    ensures
        aligned(addr, s1 + s2),
{
}
reason: Alignment to two sizes does not imply alignment to their sum (e.g., 12 is aligned to 4 and 6 but not to 10)

name: align_result_double_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_5_align_result_double_aligned(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 1,
        b <= usize::MAX / 2,
    ensures
        aligned(align_to_usize(a, b) as nat, (b * 2) as nat),
{
}
reason: Aligning down to b does not guarantee the result is also aligned to 2b (e.g., align_to_usize(5,4)==4 but 4 is not 8-aligned)
